title: Node HTTP Primitives, Connections, Protocols, Timeouts, and Shutdown · Inference Brief: Protocol Transfer Evidence
summary: A Node server inherits defaults without understanding header, request, keep-alive, upgrade, idle-socket, HTTP/2, or graceful-close behavior. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts protocol transfer evidence increment in Node HTTP server protocol and timeout budget. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Require live TLS identity, proxy, slow-client, protocol negotiation, upgrade, drain, and packet-level evidence beyond deterministic browser models.
Separate server.close, closeIdleConnections, closeAllConnections, upgraded sockets, active responses, and forced shutdown.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Protocol Transfer Evidence · Theory Protocol Transfer Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 1066 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate protocol support with explicit header, request, and drain policy; preserve response ownership after a client abort; then remove one trusted proxy hop and recompute authority from the socket boundary. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Protocol Transfer Evidence contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Predict Protocol Transfer Evidence · Theory Protocol Transfer Evidence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for require live tls identity, proxy, slow-client, protocol negotiation, upgrade, drain, and packet-level evidence beyond deterministic browser models. For Node and Express server Predict Protocol Transfer Evidence · Theory Protocol Transfer Evidence, keep Protocol Transfer Evidence connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
